name: ecodev-front

metadata.name: ecodev-front
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Application Frameworks
Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
# EcoDev Front

This library is a reduced and opinionated version of Dash Mantine Components [DMC](https://www.dash-mantine-components.com/), itself a simplified version of the React Mantine library. It includes some basic functionalities such as
customisable components, navbar menus and customised component builders.

Full documentation of the library can be found [here](https://ecodev-doc.lcabox.com/libraries/front/)

modules.name: ecodev_front.modal
documentation: Component which to display a dmc.Modal on the side of the screen

functions.name: ecodev_front.modal.modal
documentation: Returns a dmc.Modal which can be used to display content on the screen temporarily

modules.name: ecodev_front.graphs.subplots
documentation: Module implementing how to create subplots in plotly

functions.name: ecodev_front.graphs.subplots.subplots
documentation:
Function to create subplots
- rows: number of rows
- cols: number of columns
- shared_xaxes: bool to indicate if you want to have a single x axis
- row_titles: list of titles for each row
- subplot_titles: list of titles for each subplot
- specs: list of lists where you can define the plot type used
Some graphs (Sunburst and Pie Chart) need to have their type specified to generate the subplot
- vertical_spacing: spacing between rows in the figure (0-1)

functions.name: ecodev_front.graphs.subplots.subplots_y_axis_labels
documentation: Adds a y-axis label to a specific row and column

modules.name: ecodev_front.graphs.scatter
documentation: Module to render a go.Scatter plotly graph

functions.name: ecodev_front.graphs.scatter.scatter
documentation:
Function to render a scatter plot
 - x: information that will be at the bottom of the graph (can be dates, aggregations, etc...)
 - y: numerical values that will be aggregated
 - text: list of strings to be displayed on the scatter points
 - textfont: dictionary to customize the font of the text
 - mode: what kind of points will be displayed
 - color: custom color to be applied to the pie chart. Plotly default colors will
 be applied if not filled in
 - line_width: width of the line connecting the data points
 - fill: (tonexty) fills the area under the curve
 - textposition: determines where the text should be placed,
 - customdata: list of str that represent a text we want to use in the hovertemplate
 - hovertemplate: str that must be formatted like html, that will show when you hover on the
 graph
 - name: str that will be displayed in the legend
 - legendgroup: str when you want to group legend items
 - stackgroup: str to indicate which lines you want to stack together
 - show_legend: bool to show or hide the legend for this trace

modules.name: ecodev_front.graphs.legend_position
documentation: Module implementing different legend positioning

functions.name: ecodev_front.graphs.pie_chart.pie_chart
documentation:
Function to render a pie chart
 - labels: column on which we want to perform an aggregation
 - values: numerical values that will be aggregated
 - customdata: list of str that represent a text we want to use in the hovertemplate
 - text: list of str that will be displayed in the graph
 - hovertemplate: str that must be formatted like html, that will show when you hover on the
 graph
 - colors: list of custom colors to be applied to the pie chart. Plotly default colors will
 be applied if not filled in
 - text_threshold: float between 0 and 1, that controls the minimum threshold in terms of
 percentage of the total under which category labels will be shown. The higher the threshold,
 the less categories will be shown (only the biggest one),

modules.name: ecodev_front.graphs.graph
documentation: Dash app component to display a graph with correct sizing / padding.

functions.name: ecodev_front.graphs.graph.graph_box
documentation: Helper component to format a plotly figure with correct sizing & padding.

modules.name: ecodev_front.graphs.fig_helpers
documentation: Functions to interact with plotly graph objects

functions.name: ecodev_front.graphs.fig_helpers.hide_duplicate_legends
documentation: Hides duplicates legend

functions.name: ecodev_front.graphs.bar_chart.bar_chart
documentation:
Function to render a bar chart
 - x: list of x-value which will also be shown as x-axis labels
 - y: numerical values that will be aggregated
 - orientation: ('h'|'v') determines whether the chart will be displayed horizontally or
 vertically
 - customdata: list of str that represent a text we want to use in the hovertemplate
 - text: list of str that will be displayed in the graph
 - hovertemplate: str that must be formatted like html, that will show when you hover on the
 graph
 - colors: list of custom colors to be applied to the pie chart. Plotly default colors will
 be applied if not filled in
 - textposition: determines where the text should be placed,
 - name: str that will be displayed in the legend
 - legendgroup: str when you want to group legend items

modules.name: ecodev_front.group
documentation: Module to render dmc.Group elements

functions.name: ecodev_front.group.group
documentation: Renders a dmc.Group component with the correct formatting

functions.name: ecodev_front.graphs.fig_layout.apply_fig_layout
documentation:
Function to configure layout options on a plotly figure
- fig: the figure on which you want to apply the layout
- title: str that will be the title of the graph
- barmode: determines how bars are displayed in a grouped bar chart
- annotation_text: text that will be displayed at the center of the pie chart
- show_legend: bool, whether to display the legend or not (default: False)
- legend: controls the orientation of the legend
- margins: control the spacing on the sides of the graph
- minsize: minimum font size (elements under this size would not be displayed)

functions.name: ecodev_front.graphs.sunburst.sunburst
documentation:
Function to render a sunburst
 - ids: a list of unique ids
 - labels: the text that will appear on the graph for each section
 - parents: a list of ids of each parent node
 - values: a list of numerical values
 - hovertemplate: str that must be formatted like html, that will show when you hover on the
 graph
 - customdata: list of str that represent a text we want to use in the hovertemplate
 - textinfo: the format of the string that will be displayed on the graph
 - mode: what kind of points will be displayed
 - color: custom color to be applied to the pie chart. Plotly default colors will
 be applied if not filled in
 - textposition: determines where the text should be placed,
 - branchvalues: whether you want the sunburst graph to be completed 360 or not (the sum of
 children values must be equal to the parent value)
 - name: str that will be displayed in the legend
 - colors: list of custom colors to be applied to the pie chart. Plotly default colors will
 be applied if not filled in
 - insidetextorientation: str to indicate how the text will be displayed

functions.name: ecodev_front.graphs.sunburst.get_formatted_data_sunburst
documentation:
Returns four lists which will be used in the Sunburst chart
  - ids: a list of unique ids
  - labels: the text that will appear on the graph for each section
  - parents: a list of ids of each parent node
  - values: a list of numerical values
Arguments:
    - data: a dict
    - level_1_col: the column name for the first level of the sunburst chart
    - level_2_col: the column name for the second level of the sunburst chart
    - value_col: the column name for the numerical value (default is VALUE)
    - unit: the unit for the numerical value (default is '')

modules.name: ecodev_front.alert

functions.name: ecodev_front.alert.alert
documentation:
Returns an alert (text specifically formatted with a title, a message and a background color)
- title: text that will be displayed on the top of the alert
- message: text that will be displayed in the body of the alert
- color: color of the alert background (e.g., 'green', 'red', 'blue')

functions.name: ecodev_front.button.button
documentation:
Function to render a button
- id: unique identifier for the button
- text: text displayed on the button
- icon: icon displayed on the left side of the button
- variant: ('subtle', 'gradient', 'filled', 'light', 'outline') format of the button
- color: color of the button
- full_width: whether the button should take up full width of its parent container
- w: width of the button in pixels (optional)
